# Test Fixtures Manifest

Each fixture pair under `tests/fixtures/<name>/` consists of `before.kdbx` and
`after.kdbx`. They are committed to the repo so a fresh `git clone` brings the
full test apparatus along.

This manifest answers four questions per fixture: **origin**, **master
password**, **semantic contents**, and **regeneration**.

## Common conventions

- **Master password (all synthetic fixtures):** `test-password-do-not-use`
- **Generator binary:** `cargo run --features cli --bin gen-fixtures`
  - Writes all known fixture pairs in one shot, deterministically (modulo the
    `keepass = "0.12"` API limitation that Entry/Group UUIDs are not settable
    by callers, so UUIDs vary across regenerations).
- **DO NOT** use these fixtures with anything resembling real data. The master
  password is published; the contents are throwaway test material.

## Active fixtures

### `empty/`

| | |
|---|---|
| Origin | `gen-fixtures` (synthetic) |
| Master password | `test-password-do-not-use` |
| Contents | Both files: a fresh empty KDBX 4 database. No entries, no groups beyond Root. |
| Regen | `cargo run --features cli --bin gen-fixtures` |

Used by: `integration.rs::same_file_diffed_against_itself_exits_zero`,
several baseline tests across other suites.

### `add_entry/`

| | |
|---|---|
| Origin | `gen-fixtures` (synthetic) |
| Master password | `test-password-do-not-use` |
| Contents | `before`: empty database. `after`: same database with one entry added under Root: `Title=Chase`, `UserName=alice@example.com`, `Password=hunter2` (protected), `URL=https://chase.com`. |
| Regen | `cargo run --features cli --bin gen-fixtures` |

Used by: `integration.rs::add_entry_pair_exits_one_and_reports_added`,
`json_format_produces_valid_json`, `textconv_emits_dump_for_one_file`,
several tests in `git_driver.rs` and `remote_roundtrip.rs`.

### `password_change/`

| | |
|---|---|
| Origin | `gen-fixtures` (synthetic) |
| Master password | `test-password-do-not-use` |
| Contents | Both files: one entry under Root with the same Title, UserName, URL. The Password (protected field) differs between `before` and `after`. |
| Regen | `cargo run --features cli --bin gen-fixtures` |

Used by: `integration.rs::password_change_masks_default`,
`show_secrets_reveals_plaintext`, several determinism and git-driver tests.

## Pending fixtures (not yet generated)

The following are referenced in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-27-diff-kdbx.md`
but not yet generated. Tests that depend on them are marked `#[ignore]` until
the fixtures land. Each requires either an extension to `gen-fixtures` or a
manual KeePassXC procedure:

- `move_entry/`: same UUID at different paths between snapshots.
- `tag_diff/`: tags added and removed.
- `attachment_change/`: attachment content swapped (same name, different bytes).
- `history_grew/`: entry edited, previous version pushed to history.
- `cross_version/`: KDBX 3.1 vs KDBX 4.0 (tests the cross-version warning).
- `noisy_resave/`: KeePassXC-generated re-save with no semantic changes.
  Originally intended to validate noise suppression. Real-world testing
  showed KeePassXC's `Copy As` is metadata-clean, so this fixture would
  need to be generated by *opening and viewing* an entry in KeePassXC
  (which bumps `UsageCount`/`LastAccessTime`) before saving. Procedure
  is manual and platform-dependent.
- `remove_entry/`: entry present in `before`, absent in `after`.

When adding a new fixture, also add an entry to this manifest with all four
provenance fields.

## Provenance categories

- **synthetic (gen-fixtures):** built programmatically via the keepass-rs
  builder API. Deterministic in content; UUIDs vary per regeneration due to
  upstream API limitation. Use these as the default for any new test.
- **KeePassXC-Copy-As:** captured from a real KeePassXC build. Used when a
  scenario can't be expressed via the keepass-rs builder API (e.g.,
  cross-version, real-world `noisy_resave`). Regeneration procedure must be
  documented per fixture.
- **External:** sourced from third parties (test vectors, real KDBX from a
  user, etc.). Document origin with attribution. Avoid unless necessary.
